2022-09-01.title: Debt-Stricken Sri Lanka Reaches Initial Deal for I.M.F. Bailout
2022-09-01.abstract: The package includes nearly $3 billion in loans for an island nation that ousted its president amid a devastating economic crisis.

2022-09-01.title: Pencil, chalk and first-aid kits: Ukrainian children return to school in the midst of war.
2022-09-01.abstract: An estimated 2.8 million of the nation’s six million children have been forced from their homes because of the war. If they are fortunate enough to find a seat in a classroom, it will probably be in an unfamiliar town or city.

2022-09-01.title: China Locks Down Major Southern City of Chengdu
2022-09-01.abstract: The drastic response to a rise in Covid cases adds to the pressures facing Sichuan Province, which already had been dealing with drought, heat and wildfires.

2022-09-01.title: The Anglican Church’s ‘Kick in the Guts’ to Gay Parishioners
2022-09-01.abstract: Divisions over the acceptance of homosexuality have proved intractable both on a global level and inside even liberal-leaning countries like New Zealand.

2022-09-01.title: ‘We Are Not Stopping.’ U.N. Team Continues to Nuclear Plant
2022-09-01.abstract: Rafael M. Grossi, the International Atomic Energy Agency’s secretary general, told reporters he had been briefed on the increased shelling in the region between the Russian and Ukrainian armies.

2022-09-02.title: Argentina’s Vice President Unharmed After Assassination Attempt
2022-09-02.abstract: A man tried to fire a gun inches from the head of Cristina Fernández de Kirchner, the country’s most prominent leader, but it did not go off.

2022-09-02.title: ‘Condemned to Lead’ Europe, German Chancellor Struggles for Impact
2022-09-02.abstract: Facing its worst crisis since World War II, Europe is looking for leadership. Olaf Scholz is trying to take up the role, but faces a tepid response.

2022-09-02.title: Behind the Scenes of an Afghan (Australian) Soccer Story
2022-09-02.abstract: The story of a keeper and her teammates, as they fled and found new lives.

2022-09-02.title: The U.N.’s nuclear agency is limited in its ability to enforce its recommendations on Zaporizhzhia.
2022-09-02.abstract: Five remaining inspectors from a U.N. watchdog agency were assessing the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant in southern Ukraine on Friday, a day after making a risky and long-awaited trip to the imperiled facility to ensure critical emergency equipment was operational. But it remained unclear what steps the agency would be able to take to prevent the dangers of fierce combat around the plant.

2022-09-02.title: The Contentious Vote in Chile That Could Transform Indigenous Rights
2022-09-02.abstract: The proposed constitution would enshrine some of the world’s most extensive Indigenous rights. But those reforms have become the focal point of the campaign to reject the new text.

2022-09-03.title: Explosion Kills 7 Police Officers in Colombia
2022-09-03.abstract: Dozens of police officers have been killed in the country so far this year, as its new president struggles to quell a wave of violence he campaigned on ending.

2022-09-03.title: ‘We Do Not Want Unknown Graves’: The Struggle to Identify Bucha’s Victims
2022-09-03.abstract: No longer able to defer burial, Ukrainian investigators are photographing unclaimed and unidentified bodies, taking DNA samples and assigning them numbers, hoping that one day their families will claim them.

2022-09-03.title: Sri Lanka’s Ex-President Returns Home, Just Weeks After Being Ousted
2022-09-03.abstract: Gotabaya Rajapaksa’s fall from grace had seemed to end a powerful dynasty, but his arrival in the country’s capital, Colombo, shows how deeply entrenched his family is.

2022-09-03.title: Russians Mourn Gorbachev in Silent Protest Against an Absent Putin
2022-09-03.abstract: At the funeral of Mikhail S. Gorbachev, the last Soviet leader, the size of the crowds signaled the depth of the regret about the undoing of the freedoms that his tenure initiated.

2022-09-03.title: How Fake GPS Coordinates Are Leading to Lawlessness on the High Seas
2022-09-03.abstract: A technology enabling the transmission of fake locations to carry out murky or even illegal business operations could have profound implications for the enforcement of international law.

2022-09-04.title: China Threatens Reaction After U.S. Announces Arms Sales to Taiwan
2022-09-04.abstract: A $1.1 billion package would “severely jeopardize” ties, Chinese officials say in demanding that Washington revoke the deal.

2022-09-04.title: In South Korea, a Hit Show Brings Autism Into the Spotlight
2022-09-04.abstract: “Extraordinary Attorney Woo” has raised awareness of autism in South Korea, where developmental disabilities are still a taboo subject for many people.

2022-09-04.title: Russia’s Unfounded Claims of Secret U.S. Bioweapons Linger On and On
2022-09-04.abstract: Without providing evidence, the Kremlin is pressing allegations that the U.S. operates labs in Ukraine and beyond. This week, it has called an investigative session in Geneva.

2022-09-04.title: 5 Palestinians, 2 Accused of Spying for Israel, Are Executed in Gaza, Officials Say
2022-09-04.abstract: The Gaza Interior Ministry said that three of the executions were carried out by hanging and the other two by firing squad.

2022-09-04.title: New Titanic Footage Heralds Next Stage in Deep-Sea Tourism
2022-09-04.abstract: New, high-resolution footage of the Titanic offers a glimpse into the ambitions of the deep-sea tourism industry. Tourists paid $250,000 to take a submersible to view the wreckage.

2022-09-05.title: Suspects at Large After Deadly Knife Attacks in Canada
2022-09-05.abstract: The Canadian authorities identified two suspects after killings on an Indigenous reservation and in a nearby village.

2022-09-05.abstract: With 50,000 Ukrainians now finding sanctuary in Ireland, the largest number of refugees the country has ever accepted is merging with locals in homogeneous rural villages.

2022-09-05.abstract: The two men sought in the stabbing attacks that began in the James Smith Cree Nation remain at large and “are likely” in Regina, the city’s police chief, Evan Bray, said in a video posted on Twitter late Sunday night.

2022-09-05.title: South Korea Braces for Typhoon Hinnamnor
2022-09-05.abstract: The storm, expected to make landfall on Tuesday, could be the strongest in the country’s recorded history. Japan and China were also making preparations.

2022-09-05.abstract: For nearly 70 years, Stratford, Ontario, has attracted legions of theater fanatics to its Shakespeare festival. About a dozen years ago, a very different type of pilgrim began arriving: Beliebers.

2022-09-06.abstract: Typhoon Hinnamnor made landfall in South Korea, bringing record winds and dumping as much as 24 inches of rain on some parts of the country.

2022-09-06.abstract: The Ministry of Defense said a division of the South Korean Marines in Pohang had dispatched firefighters in two amphibious assault vehicles to rescue flood victims from Typhoon Hinnamnor.

2022-09-06.title: ‘Nothing Has Really Changed’: In Moscow, the Fighting Is a World Away

2022-09-06.title: In farewell speech, Boris Johnson pledges to support Liz Truss ‘every step of the way.’
2022-09-06.abstract: The outgoing prime minister defended his three-year tenure but appealed to his party to unite around his successor.

2022-09-06.abstract: Boris Johnson is expected to deliver his final address as prime minister, kicking off a day of events that will see the political office transfer to his successor, Liz Truss.

2022-09-07.title: How Will Ukraine Rebuild (and Who Should Pay)?
2022-09-07.abstract: A report to Western donors before a major reconstruction conference next month urges quick decisions, phased investment and strict foreign oversight of any aid.

2022-09-07.title: Europe Is Sacrificing Its Ancient Forests for Energy
2022-09-07.abstract: Governments bet billions on burning timber for green power. The Times went deep into one of the continent’s oldest woodlands to track the hidden cost.

2022-09-07.abstract: As vintners around the world battle extreme heat and climate change, the pioneers producing wine in Israel’s arid south are testing ideas that might soon find global application.

2022-09-07.abstract: Why are some Chinese youth dressing like middle-aged civil servants? It might be ironic, or a longing for stability in uncertain times.

2022-09-07.abstract: Blackpool contains one of the most deprived areas to turn to the Conservatives in Britain’s last general election. But with costs rising, there are already signs of cracks in that support.

2022-09-08.title: His Bees Fly to the Front Line. Soon, It May Come to Him.
2022-09-08.abstract: With nearby sunflower fields unplanted and rockets overhead, a Ukrainian’s bees ventured toward the battlefront and returned with a bumper crop.

2022-09-08.title: Second Suspect in Canada Killings Dies After Capture
2022-09-08.abstract: After a four-day manhunt, the second of two brothers sought by the Royal Canadian Mounted Police after a stabbing rampage in Saskatchewan died after he was taken into police custody, the authorities said.

2022-09-08.abstract: The prime minister claims the country can’t afford to hold national elections next year. His opponents see a power grab linked to Beijing’s influence.

2022-09-09.title: As Crises Mount, Europe Turns Once Again to Big Spending
2022-09-09.abstract: The standoff with Russia over Ukraine and rising energy prices are rapidly overturning European economic orthodoxy, with barely a peep of dissent.

2022-09-10.abstract: The southern counteroffensive has been a painstaking battle of river crossings, with pontoon bridges as prime targets for both sides. But so far, it is Ukraine that has advanced.

2022-09-10.title: These maps show how Ukraine is rapidly advancing around Izium.

The breakthrough — possibly some 50 kilometers in just a few days — threatened to encircle Russian forces, which appeared to be caught by surprise.

2022-09-10.abstract: William Ruto built a career on beating the odds. In his greatest triumph, he is expected to be inaugurated as Kenya’s fifth president on Tuesday.

2022-09-11.abstract: Drones are increasingly testing Taiwan’s capacity to respond to China's growing military pressure. Soldiers shot one down recently and are ramping up defenses.

2022-09-13.abstract: As he took the reins of power, William Ruto promised to alleviate rising prices and strengthen the judiciary. But his team limited local television access, raising concern about press freedoms.

2022-09-14.abstract: Xi Jinping, who is expected to meet with Vladimir Putin, will seek to project confidence as a global statesman at a time of grave challenges at home.

2022-09-14.abstract: A nationwide shortage of nurses has caused dozens of emergency rooms across Canada to close temporarily and forced some patients to wait days for a bed.

2022-09-15.abstract: The children’s bodies were found in suitcases that had been in a storage facility, said the New Zealand police, who are seeking the woman’s extradition on murder charges.

2022-09-16.abstract: A new documentary goes some way to explaining why the city looks the way it does.

2022-09-17.abstract: Mahsa Amini was detained for allegedly violating dress rules. Her suspicious death, publicized by social media, has led to protests and condemnations by prominent Iranians.

2022-09-17.title: Map: Tracking Typhoon Nanmadol
2022-09-17.abstract: A map showing the storm’s path as it heads toward Japan.

2022-09-17.abstract: Scientists aim to determine whether a predator population can be restored in a place where it was hunted into extinction.

2022-09-17.abstract: The U.N. General Assembly voted to allow Ukraine’s president to address the gathering of world leaders with a recorded message next week.

2022-09-18.abstract: A southeastern county was shaken by a strong temblor for the second day in a row.

2022-09-18.abstract: Fighting last week, the deadliest between the two countries since 2020, was “initiated by the Azeris,” Speaker Nancy Pelosi said, while also calling for a negotiated settlement.

2022-09-20.abstract: The country’s economy has been very hard hit by cascading crises which have disrupted worldwide trade.

2022-09-21.abstract: Ferdinand E. Marcos placed the country under military rule 50 years ago this week. With his son now in power, those who lived through those dark days fear their stories will be lost.

2022-09-21.abstract: In a rare address, which was prerecorded, President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia announced a partial mobilization of his military, effective immediately, stoking speculation that Mr. Putin could officially declare war and a nationwide draft.

2022-09-21.abstract: “At least 95 percent will die, because the ocean’s just so fierce,” said a boat skipper on the scene, where 470 whales were also beached in 2020.

2022-09-22.abstract: In towns reclaimed from Russian occupation, Ukrainian officials are working to identify — and punish — those who helped the enemy. It’s dividing some communities.

2022-09-23.abstract: As the storm approached Bermuda, officials urged residents to prepare for strong winds, rain and surf.

2022-09-23.abstract: Peregrine falcons nesting in the city’s financial district offer riveting theater every day with their daily life, including sex, death and high-speed chases.

2022-09-23.abstract: Hong Kong, Taiwan and Japan have relaxed their pandemic rules, as they look to bolster their economies and play catch-up with much of the world.

2022-09-23.title: In Myanmar, Grief and Fury After an Attack on a School
2022-09-23.abstract: Eleven children died when soldiers fired on the school, where they said rebels had taken cover. “This is a war crime,” said a U.N. expert.

2022-09-24.title: Zelensky tells Ukrainians living under occupation to avoid conscription ‘by any means.’
2022-09-24.abstract: With Russia laying the groundwork to formally annex areas it is occupying, President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine directly asked Ukrainians to help the nation’s war effort even from within the occupied territory.

2022-09-24.title: Defying travel warnings, Hasidic Jewish pilgrims flock to Ukraine.
2022-09-24.abstract: Tens of thousands of Hasidic Jews traditionally travel to Uman to mark Rosh Hashana with a visit to the burial site of a revered 18th-century rabbi.

2022-09-24.title: Iran Protests Surge to Dozens of Cities
2022-09-24.abstract: Iranians fed up with oppressive rules and a battered economy have faced bullets, tear gas and arrests to demand an end to the Islamic Republic’s rule.

2022-09-24.title: Italian Voters Appear Ready to Turn a Page for Europe
2022-09-24.abstract: With the hard-right candidate Giorgia Meloni ahead before Sunday’s election, Italy could get its first leader whose party traces its roots to the wreckage of Fascism.

2022-09-24.title: A Year When Everything Is Going Right for Western Canada’s Farmers
2022-09-24.abstract: This year’s harvest will be bountiful, with prices more than offsetting increased costs for items like fuel.

2022-09-25.title: North Korea Launches Ballistic Missile Off Its East Coast
2022-09-25.abstract: The exercise was Pyongyang’s first ballistic missile test in nearly four months and came days after the U.S.S. Ronald Reagan arrived in a South Korean port.

2022-09-25.title: The New India: Expanding Influence Abroad, Straining Democracy at Home
2022-09-25.abstract: As India rises, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has faced little pushback as he weaponizes institutions to consolidate power and entrench his Hindu nationalist vision.

2022-09-25.title: Zelensky calls on Russians to resist Putin’s conscription.
2022-09-25.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine encouraged Russians to resist their country’s conscription drive on Saturday, his latest effort to blunt President Vladimir V. Putin’s attempts to broaden the war.

2022-09-25.title: Super Typhoon Noru Heads for Philippines After ‘Explosive’ Surge
2022-09-25.abstract: Forecasters warned of heavy rains and winds that could bring flooding and landslides to densely populated areas of the country.

2022-09-25.title: Deadlier Than the Titanic: A Ferry Set Out With About 1,900 Aboard. Only 64 Survived.
2022-09-25.abstract: Tourists, vendors, babies, hundreds of university students and a soccer team from Senegal perished in a little-known ferry disaster 20 years ago. Survivors and families of victims still want justice.

2022-09-26.title: To Defend Democracy, Is Brazil’s Top Court Going Too Far?
2022-09-26.abstract: Brazil’s Supreme Court has acted as the primary check on President Jair Bolsonaro’s power. Now many are worried the court is posing its own threat.

2022-09-26.title: A recruitment officer was wounded in the latest attack on a Russian draft office.
2022-09-26.abstract: A gunman opened fire at a draft office in a Siberian town, in what is believed to be the first attack on a military recruitment center since February to result in serious injury.

2022-09-26.title: At Least 15 Killed in School Shooting in Russia, Including Children
2022-09-26.abstract: An unidentified gunman entered a school in the city of Izhevsk, killing four adults and 11 children, according to federal investigators.

2022-09-26.title: Europe Looks at Italy’s Meloni With Caution and Trepidation
2022-09-26.abstract: Giorgia Meloni, poised to be the country’s first far-right leader since Mussolini, says she supports Ukraine and has moderated her harsh views on Europe, but there are doubts, given her partners.

2022-09-26.title: Putin Ally Acknowledges Founding Wagner Mercenary Group
2022-09-26.abstract: Yevgeny V. Prigozhin, a Russian businessman and close associate of President Vladimir V. Putin, confirmed his role weeks after he was seen in a recruitment video for the private military company.

2022-09-27.title: Videos Show Women at Forefront of Iran Protests
2022-09-27.abstract: Female protesters burned their legally required head scarves and cut their hair to protest the death of Mahsa Amini, who died in police custody after being arrested for failing to cover her hair modestly enough.

2022-09-27.title: Cuba Approves Same-Sex Marriage in Historic Vote
2022-09-27.abstract: The sweeping 100-page law will also allow same-sex couples to adopt and includes measures that expand protections for women, children and the elderly.

2022-09-27.title: As referendums end, a declaration of Russian annexation is expected to follow.
2022-09-27.abstract: The Russian-led referendums in occupied parts of Ukraine are expected to show that a majority of residents voted to join Russia, with the Kremlin then formally announcing annexation as soon as this week.

2022-09-27.title: Wall Street Remains Unsteady After a Painful Stretch for Investors
2022-09-27.abstract: Concerns about rising interest rates and the prospects of a global slowdown remain.

2022-09-27.title: Leaks in undersea gas pipelines from Russia to Germany after blasts raise suspicions of sabotage.
2022-09-27.abstract: The authorities in Germany, Denmark and Sweden are investigating the cause of leaks in two gas pipelines under the Baltic Sea that caused an abrupt drop in pressure on Monday.

2022-09-28.title: Pipeline breaks look deliberate, Europeans say, exposing vulnerability in vital energy infrastructure.
2022-09-28.abstract: BERLIN — Explosions under the Baltic Sea and the rupture of major natural gas pipelines from Russia to Germany appeared to be a deliberate attack, officials across Europe said on Tuesday, deepening uncertainty about European energy security amid soaring prices and fears of running short of fuel over the winter.

2022-09-28.title: Zambia and Its New President Are Still on Their Honeymoon
2022-09-28.abstract: Hakainde Hichilema is hailed as a miracle worker for turning around the fortunes of the southern African country. But with his opposition neutralized and a corruption drive focused on his predecessors, some are uneasy.

2022-09-28.title: Far From Routine, Asia Trip Presents Thorny Tests for Kamala Harris
2022-09-28.abstract: A visit for Shinzo Abe’s controversial state funeral also includes outreach to Asian allies over military advances by North Korea and China.

2022-09-28.title: The top E.U. diplomat says the Nord Stream leaks resulted from ‘a deliberate act.’
2022-09-28.abstract: The leaks in the Nord Stream gas pipelines under the Baltic Sea resulted from “a deliberate act,” the European Union’s top diplomat said on Wednesday, joining a growing chorus of European leaders in speaking of sabotage.

2022-09-28.title: Sex Assault Trial in U.S. a Rare Moment for Chinese #MeToo Movement
2022-09-28.abstract: Richard Liu, also known as Liu Qiangdong, will be one of the few high-profile Chinese figures to face a courtroom jury over sexual assault allegations.

2022-09-29.title: Myanmar Sentences Australian Economic Adviser to Three Years
2022-09-29.abstract: Sean Turnell, arrested five days after the military seized power in a coup, has been convicted by the junta of violating the country’s official secrets act.

2022-09-29.title: On Eve of Election, Bolsonaro’s Party Attacks Brazil’s Voting Systems
2022-09-29.abstract: Four days before Brazilians vote, President Jair Bolsonaro’s political party formally claimed, without evidence, that government employees could alter ballots.

2022-09-29.title: Despite Iran’s Efforts to Block Internet, Technology Has Helped Fuel Outrage
2022-09-29.abstract: Online, Iranians engage in a world their leaders don’t want them to see.

2022-09-29.title: Brazil’s Favorite Leftist Is Back From Prison and Trying to Defeat Bolsonaro
2022-09-29.abstract: Former President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is trying to cap a stunning political comeback by defeating the incumbent Jair Bolsonaro in Brazil’s elections on Sunday.

2022-09-29.title: The Last Days of an Outback Town Where Every Breath Can Be Toxic
2022-09-29.abstract: The town of Wittenoom, ruined by asbestos mining, will be wiped off the face of the earth. But first the government had to remove those who wanted to stay.

2022-09-30.title: Putin is expected to illegally annex Russian-occupied parts of Ukraine following sham votes.
2022-09-30.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia is expected on Friday to declare that four regions in eastern and southern Ukraine will become part of Russia, as the Kremlin seeks to solidify its tenuous hold over parts of the country in an illegal annexation that has been widely denounced.

2022-09-30.title: Countering China, the U.S. Signs a Broad Deal to Aid Pacific Nations
2022-09-30.abstract: Beijing’s inroads in a strategic region are pushing the Biden administration to shore up withered ties.

2022-09-30.title: Ukraine says 25 people are dead in an attack on a civilian convoy in Zaporizhzhia.
2022-09-30.abstract: Russia launched a flurry of strikes, creating scenes of destruction inside Ukraine as the Kremlin planned an elaborate, and widely dismissed, annexation ceremony.

2022-09-30.title: French Feminist Rabbi Captivates Multifaith Crowds With Musings on Mortality
2022-09-30.abstract: With Paris locked down over Passover, a rabbi started holding weekly talks over Zoom about Jewish texts. Thousands have tuned in to hear her reflections on death. “She is my rabbi,” said an atheist.

2022-09-30.title: Even as Iranians Rise Up, Protests Worldwide Are Failing at Record Rates
2022-09-30.abstract: Mass protests, once a grave threat to even the fiercest autocrat, have plummeted in effectiveness, a study shows. Factors appear to include polarization, social media and rising nationalist attitudes.
